meta.title: SF-SPAP-2: Platform Operations, Security, and Collaboration

questions.stem: Which Snowflake layer is responsible for authentication, metadata management, and query optimization?
questions.explanation: Cloud Services coordinates authentication, metadata, and optimization. Storage persists data, warehouses provide compute, and Marketplace distributes data products.

questions.stem: A team needs more query concurrency without increasing stored data capacity. What should they scale?
questions.explanation: Compute and storage are decoupled in Snowflake, so warehouse sizing addresses concurrency and execution capacity without changing persisted storage.

questions.stem: Which statement BEST describes Snowflake micro-partitions?

options.text: They are user-managed partitions defined with DDL
They are contiguous units of columnar storage managed automatically by Snowflake
They are temporary cache blocks stored only in warehouses
They are external files that must match one table each

questions.explanation: Snowflake automatically organizes table data into micro-partitions and maintains metadata for pruning. They are not user-managed table partitions or warehouse cache blocks.

questions.stem: An administrator wants up to 90 days of Time Travel for selected permanent tables. Which edition requirement applies?

options.text: Enterprise Edition or higher is required
Any edition works if the warehouse is XL

questions.explanation: Extended Time Travel beyond the Standard default requires Enterprise Edition or higher. Warehouse size does not unlock retention features.

questions.stem: Which statements about Snowflake objects are correct? (Select TWO)

options.text: A warehouse stores table data permanently
An organization can contain multiple accounts

questions.explanation: Databases contain schemas, and organizations can include multiple accounts. Warehouses are compute only, and roles are security principals rather than containers for tables.

questions.stem: Which client is Snowflake's command-line interface for executing SQL?
questions.explanation: SnowSQL is the CLI. Snowsight is the web UI, Snowpipe is ingestion, and Snowpark is a developer API.

questions.stem: Which stage type is managed by Snowflake and stores files inside Snowflake-managed storage?
questions.explanation: An internal named stage stores files in Snowflake-managed storage. External stages point to cloud object storage outside Snowflake.
questions.reference: [Staging data files from a local file system](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/data-load-local-file-system-stage)

questions.stem: A CSV file has a pipe delimiter and a header row. What is the BEST way to load it repeatedly with consistent parsing?

options.text: Create a file format and reference it from `COPY INTO`
Resize the warehouse before every load
Convert the file into a transient table first

questions.explanation: Reusable file formats let `COPY INTO` apply delimiter, header, and other parsing settings consistently across loads.

questions.stem: What does Snowpipe primarily provide compared with running scheduled `COPY INTO` jobs manually?

options.text: Continuous serverless ingestion of newly arrived files
A replacement for virtual warehouses in analytics
Automatic schema normalization for all sources
Automatic data sharing to other accounts

questions.explanation: Snowpipe is designed for near-continuous ingestion of new files. It does not replace compute warehouses, perform universal schema normalization, or handle sharing.

questions.stem: Which `COPY INTO` option is used to control how Snowflake handles load errors?
questions.explanation: `ON_ERROR` defines whether Snowflake continues, skips files, or aborts when load errors occur.

questions.stem: A team wants a table with lower storage cost and no Fail-safe, but they still need short-term Time Travel. Which table type fits BEST?
questions.explanation: Transient tables omit Fail-safe while still supporting limited Time Travel. Permanent tables include Fail-safe; secure is not a table type.

questions.stem: Which file formats are supported for loading data into Snowflake? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: Parquet and JSON are supported file formats. Presentation and image authoring formats are not native structured load formats for Snowflake tables.

questions.stem: Which data type is commonly used to store semi-structured JSON data in Snowflake?
questions.explanation: VARIANT stores semi-structured data such as JSON, XML, or Avro so it can be queried with SQL path notation.

questions.stem: How do you typically access the field `customer.id` inside a VARIANT column named `payload`?
questions.explanation: Snowflake commonly uses colon path notation for semi-structured traversal, with additional bracket notation when needed.

questions.stem: Which clause filters rows before grouping?
questions.explanation: `WHERE` filters rows before aggregation. `HAVING` filters grouped results after aggregation.

questions.stem: A query groups orders by region and needs to keep only regions with total sales above 1,000. Which clause should be used?
questions.explanation: Because the filter applies to aggregated totals after `GROUP BY`, `HAVING` is the correct clause.

questions.stem: Which join types are valid in Snowflake SQL? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: Snowflake supports standard SQL joins such as INNER and LEFT OUTER. PIPE JOIN and WAREHOUSE JOIN are not SQL join types.

questions.stem: What is the main benefit of storing semi-structured data in VARIANT instead of plain VARCHAR?

options.text: VARIANT allows path-based querying and preserves semi-structured types
VARIANT automatically encrypts with a stronger key
VARIANT eliminates the need for warehouses
VARIANT can only be queried from Snowpark

questions.explanation: VARIANT preserves semi-structured structure and supports direct path queries. Encryption, compute, and interface usage are separate concerns.

questions.stem: Which function is commonly used to cast a string into a timestamp in Snowflake?
questions.explanation: `TO_TIMESTAMP` converts supported string representations into timestamps.

questions.stem: Which clause determines the final presentation order of query results?
questions.explanation: `ORDER BY` sorts the final result set after the query has produced rows. `GROUP BY` groups data, but it does not guarantee final display order.

questions.stem: What access model does Snowflake use for granting privileges to users through roles?
questions.explanation: Snowflake uses role-based access control. Privileges are granted to roles, and roles are granted to users or other roles.

questions.stem: Which privilege would a role need to query a table, assuming it can already access the database and schema?
questions.explanation: To read table data the role needs `SELECT` on the table, plus required `USAGE` on parent objects.

questions.stem: Which authentication approaches are supported by Snowflake? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: Snowflake supports username/password, MFA, SSO, and key-pair authentication among other options. It does not rely on anonymous access.

questions.stem: What is the purpose of a network policy in Snowflake?

options.text: To cluster micro-partitions by IP range
To restrict connections based on allowed or blocked IP addresses
To encrypt stages with customer-managed keys

questions.explanation: Network policies control which IP addresses can connect.

questions.stem: An organization wants sensitive columns automatically obscured for some users while leaving the table structure unchanged. Which Snowflake feature fits BEST?
questions.explanation: Masking policies dynamically protect sensitive column values based on policy logic and role context.

questions.stem: What is the core benefit of Snowflake secure data sharing?

options.text: Consumers query live shared data without copying the underlying table data
Providers must export CSV files for every consumer
Data is always replicated into the consumer account
Only Marketplace listings can be shared

questions.explanation: Secure data sharing exposes live objects to consumers without physically copying the data.

questions.stem: A provider wants to share data with a partner that does not have its own Snowflake account. What is the BEST option?
questions.explanation: Reader accounts let providers share data with parties that are not already Snowflake customers.

questions.stem: What must a provider create first in order to share database objects directly with another Snowflake account?
questions.explanation: The provider creates a share and grants objects to it before the consumer can create a database from that share.

questions.stem: Which statements about Snowflake Marketplace are correct? (Select TWO)

options.text: It helps discover third-party or provider listings
It is the same thing as a virtual warehouse
It can distribute data products to consumers
It requires copying all data into every consumer account

questions.explanation: Marketplace is for discovering and distributing listings such as data products.

questions.stem: A team wants to collaborate privately with internal business units rather than publish a public listing. Which capability is MOST aligned?
questions.explanation: Data Exchange supports curated private sharing communities.

questions.stem: Which Snowflake capability lets developers use Python, Java, or Scala DataFrame-style APIs that execute in Snowflake?
questions.explanation: Snowpark is the developer framework for building data processing code that runs in Snowflake.

questions.stem: What is Streamlit in Snowflake primarily used for?

options.text: Building data apps close to governed Snowflake data
Loading files into internal stages only

questions.explanation: Streamlit in Snowflake is for interactive data applications that run close to Snowflake-managed data and governance.

questions.stem: Which statement BEST describes the Native App Framework?

options.text: It lets providers build and distribute applications on Snowflake to consumers
It is a replacement for SQL joins
It is required for every secure share

questions.explanation: The Native App Framework is for packaging and distributing apps that run on Snowflake.

questions.stem: Which tools are appropriate for Snowflake automation or integration? (Select TWO)

options.text: Snowflake REST APIs or CLI tooling

questions.explanation: Drivers, connectors, APIs, and CLI tooling support automation and integration.

questions.stem: Why can two warehouses query the same table concurrently without copying the table into each warehouse?

options.text: Warehouses persist separate table copies automatically
Snowflake decouples shared storage from independent compute clusters
Each schema is bound to exactly one warehouse
Marketplace synchronizes the table to all warehouses

questions.explanation: Snowflake uses centralized storage with independent compute clusters, so multiple warehouses can operate on the same underlying data concurrently.

questions.stem: A file landed in an external stage, but the team wants to load it into a table with managed parsing and error options. Which command is central?
questions.explanation: Snowflake loading workflows rely on staged files and `COPY INTO` with parsing and error-handling options.

questions.stem: A developer needs one row per element in a JSON array stored in VARIANT. Which Snowflake table function is commonly used?
questions.explanation: `FLATTEN` expands nested arrays or objects from semi-structured data into rows.

questions.stem: What encryption posture is provided by Snowflake by default for customer data?

options.text: No encryption unless Enterprise Edition is enabled
AES-256 at rest and TLS in transit
TLS at rest and AES-128 in transit

questions.explanation: Snowflake documents strong default encryption at rest and TLS for data in transit.

questions.stem: When a consumer creates a database from a provider share, where does the provider continue to manage the source objects?
questions.explanation: The provider continues managing the shared source objects in the provider account.

questions.stem: A team wants governed SQL dashboards, app development, and data sharing all within the same platform. Which statement is MOST accurate?

options.text: Snowflake only supports SQL querying, not application or sharing capabilities
Snowflake combines core data platform features with ecosystem capabilities such as Streamlit, Native Apps, and Marketplace
Every integration requires exporting data out of Snowflake first
Snowflake disables programmatic access when Snowsight is enabled

questions.explanation: Snowflake extends beyond core warehousing with app, sharing, and integration capabilities.
